name: clock_gated_idle
description: Always-on CPU domain with aggressive RTL clock gating during idle.
notes: UPF still has one always-on domain because this scheme targets dynamic power through clock enables.
The clock-gating controls are core_clk_en and mem_clk_en in u_power_controller.

gated_in_rtl: u_core_clk_gate gates the CPU-side clock when the controller enters IDLE or LIGHT_SLEEP.
u_mem_clk_gate gates the memory-side clock when the controller enters IDLE or LIGHT_SLEEP.
The fetch PC is held during LSU stalls so load/store wait states do not repeatedly advance the front end.
estimated_behavior: Clock-gating savings are estimated with clock_gating_efficiency and activity_by_power_state.
No CPU or memory power switches are modeled for this scheme; domains remain always-on.
Retention, isolation, and switched-supply energy are not claimed as implemented behavior for this scheme.
designer_use: Use this scheme to study dynamic-power reduction from idle and stall clock gating before introducing switched domains.
Compare against dvfs_retention_domains when deciding whether leakage/recovery overhead justifies power gating.
